qa_pairs.question: How do you specify an LSTC license server using environment variables for LS-DYNA jobs?
qa_pairs.answer: To specify an LSTC license server using environment variables, you need to set `LSTC_LICENSE=network` and `LSTC_LICENSE_SERVER=<port>@<server>` in your Slurm scripts. In these variables, `<port>` is an integer number and `<server>` is the hostname or IP address of your LSTC license server.

qa_pairs.question: Is the `~/.licenses/ls-dyna.lic` file required when configuring an LSTC license server with environment variables, and how do you ensure it exists?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, even when using environment variables to specify an LSTC license server, the `~/.licenses/ls-dyna.lic` file must exist (even if empty) for any `ls-dyna` or `ls-dyna-mpi` module to successfully load. The environment variables will take priority over any values in this file. To ensure the file exists, run `touch ~/.licenses/ls-dyna.lic` once on the command line on each cluster where you plan to submit jobs.
